claims.claim: Aggregate productivity statistics will show no clear AI-driven gains through end-2026 while firm-level performance concentration keeps growing
claims.grounding: BLS/OECD productivity statistics and PwC/NBER firm-level concentration measures exist and publish on known schedules
claims.confidence_rationale: Productivity stats lag adoption by construction; concentration evidence (PwC/NBER signals) already strong. Risk: late-2026 statistical revision.
claims.update_test: [tightened 2026-06-10 grounding audit] Two conjuncts, scored explicitly: (A) BLS annual US nonfarm labor productivity growth for 2026 stays at or below 2.2% AND no BLS/OECD release attributes a stated uptick to AI; (B) at least one published 2026/early-2027 firm-level study (PwC, NBER, or equivalent) reports widening AI-performance concentration. Both hold = confirmed; exactly one = partial; neither = disconfirmed. Resolve on the 2026 annual releases, by 2027-03-31.

claims.claim: At least two more African states beyond South Africa will formally designate data centres as critical infrastructure by end-2026
claims.grounding: South Africa's designation is gazetted fact; Kenya and Nigeria have active policy processes on record
claims.confidence_rationale: SA precedent set, Kenya/Nigeria discussing; designation requires formal gazette action which moves slower than discourse.
claims.update_test: Official gazette/policy designations counted; signals corpus already tracks SA precedent

related_to.note: critical-infrastructure designation and powered-MW delivery both read the African data-centre buildout

claims.claim: The EU AI Act simplification/omnibus turn will reduce enforceable obligations for frontier deployers relative to the 2024 text by end-2026
claims.grounding: The EU AI Act omnibus is a live legislative process with a published May 7 political agreement
claims.confidence_rationale: Omnibus/simplification signals from three independent scans; deregulatory momentum visible since May.
claims.update_test: Final omnibus text vs 2024 obligations: count of softened or delayed frontier-relevant articles

evidence.note: May 7 2026 Council+Parliament political agreement on AI Omnibus: Annex III HRAIS obligations deferred Aug 2026 → Dec 2027; Annex I deferred Aug 2027 → Aug 2028.

claims.claim: The AU's governance-first Continental AI Strategy phase (2025-26) will not pull material physical-layer compute investment into existence by phase close: major African data-centre/compute projects announced through mid-2027 will cite commercial or hyperscaler logic, not AU policy instruments, as the decisive investment rationale
claims.grounding: The AU Continental AI Strategy is adopted policy; the comparison set is real announced DC investments with stated rationales
claims.confidence_rationale: Historical base rate: policy-first frameworks rarely cited as decisive in African DC siting; commercial logic dominated every 2025-26 announcement in corpus (Kasi, MTN-Genova, Equinix, MS-G42). ADAPT pilots are the live disconfirmation risk.
claims.update_test: [precedence fix 2026-06-10, Bugbot PR41] Among African data-centre/compute announcements >USD 100M or >20MW between 2025-06 and 2027-06-30: DISCONFIRMED if any names an AU/Continental AI Strategy instrument as a primary stated rationale. Otherwise PARTIAL if AU instruments appear as secondary rationale in two or more announcements. Otherwise CONFIRMED (zero primary AND fewer than two secondary). Exactly one outcome is possible for any evidence set; precedence order: disconfirmed > partial > confirmed.

claims.claim: At least one of the Big Five US tech firms will cut or flatten its AI capex guidance year-over-year by the end of Q1 2027 reporting, absent clear aggregate AI productivity gains in the 2026 statistical releases
claims.grounding: Big Five capex guidance is published quarterly in earnings filings; NBER w35290 quantifies the current curve
claims.confidence_rationale: Wachter w35290 implies the bet must show or unwind on a short fuse, but Big Five have absorbed flat productivity before without guiding down; 0.55 reflects genuine uncertainty between balance-sheet discipline and arms-race lock-in.
claims.update_test: [tightened 2026-06-10 grounding audit] Conditional branches made explicit: IF BLS 2026 annual productivity growth >= 2.5% (condition 'clear aggregate gains' met) the claim resolves UNRESOLVABLE. Otherwise: confirmed if any Big Five FY2027 AI capex guidance is cut or flat year-over-year in earnings filings through 2027-04-30; disconfirmed if all five raise guidance.

claims.claim: Kasi Cloud publicly confirms its 5.5MW Lekki phase as live/in-service by 2026-07-31
claims.grounding: Kasi Cloud is a real company with a publicly announced Q2 2026 partial-online milestone
claims.update_test: Company announcement, press coverage, or industry tracker confirming power-on/service; silence past July 31 resolves no

validity.reason: Outcome pre-dated registration: Kasi Cloud publicly commissioned the Lekki LOS1 first phase on 2026-05-19, twenty-two days before this claim was registered on 2026-06-10. A question answered before it was asked is not a forecast. Pre-registration evidence search is now mandatory for every new claim.

related_to.note: Kasi Lekki is one of the named projects in the structural claim; its 5.5MW commissioning was a direct fast reading on phase delivery

resolution.note: Kasi Cloud Datacenters formally commissioned its Lekki LOS1 campus on 2026-05-19, with the 5.5MW first phase publicly announced as operational/open. Sources: DCD (https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/nigerian-data-center-operator-kasi-opens-first-phase-of-100mw-facility/) headlined 'Kasi opens first phase'; company press release on GlobeNewswire (https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/05/19/3297917/0/en/Kasi-Cloud-Datacenters-Commissions-West-Africa-s-First-Hyperscale-Ready-AI-Capable-Data-Centre-Campus-in-Lagos.html); TechAfrica News (https://techafricanews.com/2026/05/28/kasi-cloud-datacenters-flags-off-west-africas-first-ai-ready-hyperscale-data-centre-in-lagos/); Vanguard (https://www.vanguardngr.com/2026/05/kasi-cloud-has-nigerias-first-ai-data-centre-landed/). Dated milestone publicly confirmed within window.

claims.claim: The EU AI Act omnibus simplification package receives formal Council adoption by 2026-07-31
claims.grounding: Formal Council adoption is a scheduled, public EU legislative step following the recorded May 7 agreement
claims.update_test: Council formal adoption recorded in the EU register; political agreement alone does not count

related_to.note: Council agreement on the omnibus is the first observable gate the simplification claim must pass through

evidence.note: EU Council published the provisionally agreed Digital Omnibus on AI compromise text 2026-05-13 as Council document 9247/26 (endorsing 9034/26). Multiple law firm trackers (Latham & Watkins, Gibson Dunn, White & Case) expect formal Council adoption before 2026-08-02 to avoid Annex III lapse. Evidence supports the directional claim; formal Council vote NOT YET recorded in EU register, so claim stays open and unresolved per its strict update test ('Council formal adoption recorded; political agreement alone does not count').

EU Commission press release IP_26_1024 (2026-05-07 provisional Council-Parliament agreement); Council document 9247/26 published 2026-05-13 with the agreed Digital Omnibus text. Multiple legal trackers (Gibson Dunn, White & Case, Latham & Watkins, DLA Piper) explicitly expect formal Council adoption before 2026-08-02 to avoid Annex III HRAIS lapse — directional pressure is overwhelming. Update test still strict ('formal Council adoption recorded in EU register; political agreement does not count') — claim stays open pending formal vote.

claims.claim: At least one Big Five Q2-2026 earnings call (Jul-Aug) raises full-year AI capex guidance again
claims.grounding: Q2 earnings calls are dated public events with published transcripts
claims.update_test: Earnings transcripts by 2026-08-15: any explicit upward revision of FY capex guidance tied to AI/data centres

related_to.note: a raised full-year capex guidance in Q2 earnings pulls directly against the cut-or-flatten position

claims.claim: Through 2027-06-30, governance and public-sector roles will show no measurable surge of elite talent inflows: published civil-service application volumes (UK Civil Service Fast Stream, US federal USAJOBS annual reporting) and major govtech hiring indices all stay below +25% versus their 2025 baselines
claims.grounding: Published application-volume and hiring datasets that exist and report annually; 2025 baselines are already on record
claims.confidence_rationale: Strictly harder than the withdrawn 003 (no escape clause): if a renewal trigger emerges AND reallocation follows, this now fails. Dropped from 0.80 to 0.72 accordingly.
claims.update_test: Confirmed if no listed dataset shows a +25% rise vs 2025 by 2027-06-30; disconfirmed if any does; partial if data discontinued for some but no observed surge elsewhere

claims.claim: At least one of the named 2026 African AI infrastructure projects (Kasi Cloud Lekki 100MW campus, Uganda's announced AI factory, Kenya hyperscale builds) will, by 2027-03-31, either delay its next announced phase by six or more months or have brought less than half of its announced capacity into service
claims.grounding: Named, announced projects with public capacity figures and phase timelines on record
claims.confidence_rationale: Observable version is stricter than the withdrawn mechanism-claim's 0.80: a project can stall for absorption reasons without tripping the 6-month/50% thresholds. Dropped to 0.70.
claims.update_test: Company announcements, press coverage, and industry trackers: confirmed on any 6+ month phase slip or <50% of announced capacity in service at any listed project; disconfirmed if all listed projects hit phases within 6 months and exceed half capacity

evidence.note: Kasi Cloud Lekki 5.5MW first phase commissioned 2026-05-19, on its announced Q2-2026 schedule — first named project hit its phase target without slip. One project on time does not disconfirm the umbrella claim (Uganda AI factory + Kenya hyperscale builds still untested through 2027-03-31), but this is one of three test legs falling against the slip hypothesis.

Microsoft-G42 Kenya geothermal hyperscale DC formally suspended by Pres. Ruto 2026-05-08 over 1GW power requirement vs Kenya's 3GW total installed capacity — explicit slip/scope cut at one of the three listed project legs (Kenya hyperscale builds). Combined with Kasi commissioning the other direction, the umbrella claim is now leaning confirmed via the Kenya leg.

claims.claim: Through end-2027, cumulative powered MW capacity across the named 2026 African AI data-centre projects (Kasi Cloud Lekki 100MW campus, Tetracore Ogun 20MW gas-powered DC, Uganda announced AI factory, Microsoft-G42 Kenya 1GW geothermal) will be less than 25% of cumulative announced MW capacity.
claims.grounding: Named projects: Kasi Cloud Lekki 100MW (5.5MW live as of 2026-05-19); Tetracore Ogun 20MW gas-powered DC announced March 2026 with Huawei/Inspirive; Uganda AI factory (Mar 2026 announcement); Microsoft-G42 Kenya $1B geothermal data centre (2024 announcement). DCD and Africa DC Association tracker exist today.
claims.update_test: End-2027 cumulative powered MW (per DCD industry tracker + Africa Data Centres Association annual report) divided by cumulative announced MW for the four named projects: confirmed if <25%, disconfirmed if >=25%, partial if data missing for >=2 of 4 projects.

related_to.note: restates phase delivery as a cumulative powered-MW number, measurable from public commissioning announcements

claims.claim: Through 2027-12-31, at least three jurisdictions (national or supranational) outside the European Union and ICJ will adopt explicit intergenerational-equity language in BINDING (gazetted law, regulation, or directive — not strategy paper or white paper) AI or data-centre policy.
claims.grounding: Existing anchors: EU Intergenerational Fairness Strategy (March 2026, aspirational); ICJ Advisory Opinion 32 (intergenerational-equity binding standard, 2026); EU Commissioner for Intergenerational Fairness (named office); US Indigenous Data Sovereignty Network 2026 Seventh Generation statement (non-binding). Question: does the binding form spread beyond EU + ICJ?
claims.update_test: Count of binding policy texts (laws, regulations, gazetted directives) outside EU and ICJ that explicitly invoke intergenerational equity, future generations, or seventh-generation language while regulating AI infrastructure or data centres: confirmed if >=3 from distinct jurisdictions, disconfirmed if <=2, partial if 3+ exist but at least one is purely declarative.

claims.claim: By 2027-12-31, at least two non-EU jurisdictions will publicly cite the EU AI Act 2026 omnibus simplification as precedent in their own deferral or softening of frontier-AI obligations.
claims.grounding: EU Digital Omnibus on AI: Council document 9247/26 published 2026-05-13, defers Annex III high-risk obligations 16 months and Annex I obligations 12 months. Active non-EU AI rulemaking exists in UK (AI Action Plan), US (state-level patchwork), Canada (AIDA), Singapore (Model AI Governance Framework), Korea (AI Framework Act), Brazil (PL 2338).
claims.update_test: Public regulatory document, ministerial statement, or formal consultation response from at least two jurisdictions outside the EU explicitly referencing the 2026 EU omnibus as justification or precedent for their own deferral or softening of AI obligations: confirmed if >=2 such citations identifiable in primary sources, disconfirmed otherwise.

related_to.note: third-country citation of the EU framework reads the same regulatory gravity the simplification claim bets against

claims.claim: The EU AI Office issues at least one official guidance document, FAQ update, or interpretive note explicitly addressing the Digital Omnibus on AI deferrals by 2026-08-09.
claims.grounding: EU AI Office publishes guidance on artificialintelligenceact.eu and digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu. The May 2026 omnibus (Council document 9247/26) defers Annex III HRAIS obligations 16 months. The AI Office routinely issues interpretive notes around new package adoptions.
claims.update_test: European Commission AI Office publication on digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu or EU register dated on or before 2026-08-09 that explicitly addresses how the Digital Omnibus deferrals apply: confirmed if at least one such document is identifiable, disconfirmed if none exists by 2026-08-09 EOD CET.

related_to.note: AI Office guidance volume reads the same enforcement posture the simplification claim bets on

claims.claim: At least one of the named 2026 African AI infrastructure projects (Kasi Cloud Lekki next phase above 5.5MW, Tetracore Ogun 20MW gas DC, Uganda announced AI factory, Microsoft-G42 Kenya geothermal DC) will publicly announce a delay, scope reduction, or financing gap by 2026-08-09.
claims.grounding: Named projects all exist with public commitments today. Schneider Electric and Africa DC Association have publicly flagged electricity supply as the binding constraint (Businessday NG, June 2026). DCD and TechCabal tracker activity is reliable.
claims.update_test: Any company announcement, press coverage, or industry tracker note before 2026-08-09 indicating slip, scope cut, or financing gap at any of the four named projects: confirmed if >=1 such item identifiable, disconfirmed otherwise. A celebratory progress announcement does not count.

validity.reason: Outcome pre-dated registration: the Microsoft-G42 Olkaria suspension that confirmed this claim was public on 2026-05-08, a month before registration on 2026-06-10. The resolution postmortem flagged the calibration failure; the record now also refuses the win. Pre-registration evidence search is now mandatory for every new claim.

related_to.note: 60-day milestone instrument on the same named-project set the structural claim tracks through 2027

resolution.note: RESOLVED CONFIRMED on the Kenya leg. Kenya President William Ruto publicly suspended the Microsoft-G42 Olkaria/KenGen 1GW geothermal data centre 2026-05-08, citing power capacity (1GW vs Kenya's ~3GW installed). Multiple primary sources: thinkgeoenergy.com, datacenterdynamics.com (DCD), Tom's Hardware, techweez.com, iafrica.com. The update test is unambiguous: 'Any company announcement, press coverage, or industry tracker note before 2026-08-09 indicating slip, scope cut, or financing gap.' Government suspension of a flagship B project IS a slip and scope cut. Twin confidence was 0.35 — uncalibrated against pre-registration news that had already been public for a month at claim-registration time. Calibration lesson: pre-register search depth was insufficient. Evidence URL: https://www.datacenterdynamics.com/en/news/microsoft-and-g42-data-center-in-kenya-stalled-due-to-lack-of-power-capacity/

claims.claim: The US Federal Reserve FOMC will NOT raise the federal funds rate target range at its 2026-07-28/29 meeting (hold or cut, but not raise).
claims.grounding: Federal Reserve Board publishes FOMC meeting schedule; July 28-29 2026 is on the official calendar. Decision is published 2026-07-29 at 14:00 ET in the Federal Reserve press release.
claims.update_test: Federal Reserve press release on or about 2026-07-29 reporting the FOMC decision on the federal funds rate target range: confirmed if the upper bound of the target range is equal to or lower than the upper bound set at the prior (June 2026) meeting, disconfirmed if raised.

evidence.note: Prediction markets (Polymarket, Kalshi) price 97-98% probability of Fed holding rates at the June 16-17 meeting prior to the July 28-29 meeting; markets price no rate moves rest of 2026; FOMC has held three consecutive meetings (April 29 was 8-4 hold with most dissents since 1992); Kevin Warsh installed as Chair 2026-05-15 inheriting divided committee, recent May CPI 4.2% YoY reduces cut odds. All directionally supportive of HOLD-or-CUT (the claim direction) at July 28-29; no scenario raises rates is currently priced.

claims.claim: At least one Big Five US tech firm (Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta, Microsoft) Q2-2026 calendar earnings release (reported between 2026-07-15 and 2026-08-09) will report year-over-year revenue decline OR year-over-year adjusted EPS decline.
claims.grounding: Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta, Microsoft all report Q2 calendar earnings in the last two weeks of July 2026. Press releases on each firm's investor relations site and SEC EDGAR filings. Q1 2026 cycle showed all five posting YoY revenue gains; Q2 2026 not yet reported as of 2026-06-10.
claims.update_test: Reading each of the five firms' Q2-2026 calendar earnings press releases as published between 2026-07-15 and 2026-08-09: confirmed if any of the five reports a YoY decline in either reported revenue or adjusted (non-GAAP) EPS on a single-metric basis, disconfirmed if all five report YoY gains in both metrics.

related_to.note: disclosure of capex composition shift is an early tell on the willingness to cut or flatten

claims.claim: The US Federal Reserve FOMC will hold (not raise, not cut) the federal funds rate target range at its 2026-06-16/17 meeting.
claims.grounding: FOMC June 16-17 2026 meeting on the official Federal Reserve calendar; Kevin Warsh confirmed by the Senate 2026-05-13 and took the oath as Chair 2026-05-22 (Federal Reserve press release, corrected 2026-06-11 from an earlier 05-15 error); April 29 hold was 8-4 dissents.
claims.update_test: Federal Reserve press release on or about 2026-06-17 ~14:00 ET reporting the FOMC decision: confirmed if upper bound of target range == upper bound at 2026-04-29 (3.75%); disconfirmed otherwise.

claims.claim: The ECB Governing Council will NOT cut its three key interest rates (Deposit Facility, Main Refinancing, Marginal Lending) at the 2026-07-23 monetary policy meeting.
claims.grounding: ECB Governing Council monetary policy meeting on the public ECB calendar for 2026-07-23. April 30 hold set DF 2.00%/MRO 2.15%/MLF 2.40%. Eurozone HICP still above target; some commentary suggests hikes priced for 2026 — directional pressure is hold-or-hike, not cut.
claims.update_test: ECB monetary policy statement published 2026-07-23 ~14:15 CET: confirmed if Deposit Facility Rate >= 2.00% (current level set at the April 30 2026 meeting); disconfirmed if any of the three key rates is set below its current level (DF 2.00%, MRO 2.15%, MLF 2.40%).
